{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Head of Operation",
  "description": "Hilton Garden Hotel is a hospitality company located in Enugu, dedicated to providing premium comfort, leisure, and service excellence to our guests. Our facility features a wide range of amenities, including an open bar, VIP bar, lounge, swimming pool, snooker board, table tennis board, shisha stand, and live band entertainment&mdash;all designed to create a vibrant and relaxing atmosphere. We also offer professional laundry services to ensure guest convenience. At Hilton Garden Hotel, we are passionate about hospitality and committed to delivering unforgettable experiences. We value talent and offer a supportive, growth-oriented environment for individuals looking to build rewarding careers in the hospitality industry.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Head of Operation

Job Location:&nbsp;Enugu&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	Hilton Garden Hotel, Enugu is seeking a highly experienced and strategic Head of Operations to oversee and optimize all hotel operations.
	The successful candidate will be responsible for driving operational excellence, enhancing guest satisfaction, improving profitability, and ensuring adherence to brand standards.
	This role requires a dynamic leader with strong business acumen and a passion for hospitality.


Key Responsibilities


	Oversee the day-to-day operations of all hotel departments, including Front Office, Housekeeping, Food &amp; Beverage, and Maintenance
	Develop and implement operational strategies to improve efficiency, service quality, and profitability
	Ensure compliance with Hilton brand standards, policies, and procedures
	Monitor financial performance, control costs, and drive revenue growth
	Lead, mentor, and develop department heads and staff to achieve high performance
	Establish and maintain exceptional guest service standards and handle escalated guest issues
	Coordinate with senior management on budgeting, forecasting, and business planning
	Ensure compliance with health, safety, and regulatory requirements
	Analyze operational data and prepare reports for executive decision-making.


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree or HND in Hospitality Management, Business Administration, or a related field
	Minimum of 1&ndash;3years of experience in hotel operations
	Strong leadership and team management skills
	Proven track record of improving operational performance and guest satisfaction
	Excellent financial management and analytical skills
	Strong communication, interpersonal, and problem-solving abilities
	Familiarity with hotel management systems and Microsoft Office tools.


Key Competencies


	Strategic thinking and execution
	Leadership and people management
	Financial and business acumen
	Customer experience management
	Operational efficiency and process improvement
	Decision-making and problem-solving.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;hiltongardeninnlimited@gmail.com&nbsp;using &ldquo;Head of Operations&rdquo; as the subject of the mail.",

  "validThrough": "2026-05-01",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Hilton Garden Inn"
    
  },

    "@type": "Place",

      "@type": "PostalAddress",
       "postalCode": ". ",
       "addressRegion": ". ",
       "streetAddress": ". ",
      "addressLocality": "Enugu",
      "addressCountry": "NG"
     
      
    }
  },
  "identifier": {
    "@type": "PropertyValue",
    "name": "",
    "value": "196670"
  },
  "baseSalary": {
    "@type": "MonetaryAmount",

    "value": {
      "@type": "QuantitativeValue",
      "minValue": 0,
      "maxValue": 0,

    }

Head of Operation Job at Hilton Garden Hotel




        
    

        
        Hilton Garden Hotel is a hospitality company located in Enugu, dedicated to providing premium comfort, leisure, and service excellence to our guests. Our facility features a wide range of amenities, including an open bar, VIP bar, lounge, swimming pool, snooker board, table tennis board, shisha stand, and live band entertainment—all designed to create a vibrant and relaxing atmosphere. We also offer professional laundry services to ensure guest convenience. At Hilton Garden Hotel, we are passionate about hospitality and committed to delivering unforgettable experiences. We value talent and offer a supportive, growth-oriented environment for individuals looking to build rewarding careers in the hospitality industry.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Position: Head of Operation

Job Location: Enugu 
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	Hilton Garden Hotel, Enugu is seeking a highly experienced and strategic Head of Operations to oversee and optimize all hotel operations.
	The successful candidate will be responsible for driving operational excellence, enhancing guest satisfaction, improving profitability, and ensuring adherence to brand standards.
	This role requires a dynamic leader with strong business acumen and a passion for hospitality.


Key Responsibilities


	Oversee the day-to-day operations of all hotel departments, including Front Office, Housekeeping, Food & Beverage, and Maintenance
	Develop and implement operational strategies to improve efficiency, service quality, and profitability
	Ensure compliance with Hilton brand standards, policies, and procedures
	Monitor financial performance, control costs, and drive revenue growth
	Lead, mentor, and develop department heads and staff to achieve high performance
	Establish and maintain exceptional guest service standards and handle escalated guest issues
	Coordinate with senior management on budgeting, forecasting, and business planning
	Ensure compliance with health, safety, and regulatory requirements
	Analyze operational data and prepare reports for executive decision-making.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s degree or HND in Hospitality Management, Business Administration, or a related field
	Minimum of 1–3years of experience in hotel operations
	Strong leadership and team management skills
	Proven track record of improving operational performance and guest satisfaction
	Excellent financial management and analytical skills
	Strong communication, interpersonal, and problem-solving abilities
	Familiarity with hotel management systems and Microsoft Office tools.


Key Competencies


	Strategic thinking and execution
	Leadership and people management
	Financial and business acumen
	Customer experience management
	Operational efficiency and process improvement
	Decision-making and problem-solving.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using “Head of Operations” as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Customer Service Executive",
  "description": "LVTOPSUN (Guangdong LVTOPSUN New Energy Co., Ltd.) isa professional Chinese manufacturer specializing in the research, development, production, sales, and service of&nbsp;lithium-ion energy storage batteries&nbsp;and systems, particularly for home and commercial use, offering solutions like LiFePO4 batteries, battery packs, and energy storage containers. Established in 2008, they focus on high-quality, safe, long-lasting products for solar energy integration, with business in over 50 countries.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Service Executive
Job Location:&nbsp;Amuwo Odofin, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	In Nigeria&rsquo;s growing renewable energy sector, a Customer Service Executive for a solar battery and inverter company acts as a critical link between technical sales and long-term customer satisfaction.


Key Responsibilities


	Responding promptly to inquiries via phone, WhatsApp, and email regarding solar inverter and battery issues from customers.
	Coordinating Engineers (After Sales) to resolve customers&rsquo; technical issues promptly.
	Managing customer orders, ensuring accurate data entry into systems, and coordinating with logistics teams for timely delivery.
	Providing basic troubleshooting for systems (e.g., explaining inverter codes or battery discharge rates) and escalating complex technical issues to engineers.
	Investigating and resolving complaints related to equipment performance, billing discrepancies, or supply delays professionally.
	Identifying opportunities to recommend battery upgrades or higher-capacity inverters to existing clients during support interactions.
	Proactively following up with customers post-installation to gather feedback and ensure system performance.
	Maintaining detailed records of all interactions and support tickets


Education


	BSc in Business Admnistration, Communications, or Social Sciences.
	Minimum of 3 years of working experience in customer service, preferably within the renewable energy or technology sectors


Skills:


	Familiarity with solar industry terminology, including lithium battery lifecycles and inverter load capacities.
	High empathy, problem-solving abilities, and the capacity to simplify technical information for non-technical customers.
	Fluency in English; proficiency in local languages like Hausa, Yoruba, or Igbo is often required for broader market reach.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover Letter to:&nbsp;voxaby2@yahoo.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.&nbsp;

Note: Only candidates residing close to Amuwo Odofin and its environs would be given priority.",

  "validThrough": "2026-05-12",

Customer Service Executive Job at Lvtopsun New Energy Company Limited




        
    

        
        LVTOPSUN (Guangdong LVTOPSUN New Energy Co., Ltd.) isa professional Chinese manufacturer specializing in the research, development, production, sales, and service of lithium-ion energy storage batteries and systems, particularly for home and commercial use, offering solutions like LiFePO4 batteries, battery packs, and energy storage containers. Established in 2008, they focus on high-quality, safe, long-lasting products for solar energy integration, with business in over 50 countries.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Service Executive
Job Location: Amuwo Odofin, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	In Nigeria’s growing renewable energy sector, a Customer Service Executive for a solar battery and inverter company acts as a critical link between technical sales and long-term customer satisfaction.


Key Responsibilities


	Responding promptly to inquiries via phone, WhatsApp, and email regarding solar inverter and battery issues from customers.
	Coordinating Engineers (After Sales) to resolve customers’ technical issues promptly.
	Managing customer orders, ensuring accurate data entry into systems, and coordinating with logistics teams for timely delivery.
	Providing basic troubleshooting for systems (e.g., explaining inverter codes or battery discharge rates) and escalating complex technical issues to engineers.
	Investigating and resolving complaints related to equipment performance, billing discrepancies, or supply delays professionally.
	Identifying opportunities to recommend battery upgrades or higher-capacity inverters to existing clients during support interactions.
	Proactively following up with customers post-installation to gather feedback and ensure system performance.
	Maintaining detailed records of all interactions and support tickets


Education


	BSc in Business Admnistration, Communications, or Social Sciences.
	Minimum of 3 years of working experience in customer service, preferably within the renewable energy or technology sectors


Skills:


	Familiarity with solar industry terminology, including lithium battery lifecycles and inverter load capacities.
	High empathy, problem-solving abilities, and the capacity to simplify technical information for non-technical customers.
	Fluency in English; proficiency in local languages like Hausa, Yoruba, or Igbo is often required for broader market reach.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover Letter to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email. 

Note: Only candidates residing close to Amuwo Odofin and its environs would be given priority.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

  "description": "Castville Solutions is a recruitment and business solutions provider specializing in the healthcare sector. We focus on connecting healthcare facilities with highly skilled professionals, enabling efficient operations, and driving business growth. Our services extend to recruitment, Templates for process and operations management, SOP development, and healthcare-specific training, with a clear mission to transform the healthcare workforce and operations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Account Reconciliation Officer (Hospital Operations)

Job Location:&nbsp;Ibadan, Oyo&nbsp;
Employment Type: Contract

Role Summary


	The Account Reconciliation Officer is responsible for ensuring alignment between company sales data (from the internal ERP system) and hospital client records.
	The role involves generating reports, conducting on-site validation at the hospital, resolving discrepancies, tracking payments, and maintaining accurate reconciliation records.
	The ideal candidate is detail-oriented, experienced in healthcare or hospital reconciliation, and able to manage client stakeholders effectively to ensure timely resolution and payment.


Key Responsibilities


	Data Reconciliation and Validation
	Generate and analyze sales reports from the company&rsquo;s ERP system
	Perform weekly reconciliation of internal sales data against hospital records
	Visit the hospital to access and verify client-side reports
	Compare both data sources and ensure alignment of revenue, quantities, and billing
	Identify, investigate, and resolve discrepancies
	Document all discrepancies and agreed resolutions


Payment Tracking and Follow-Up


	Track invoices based on reconciled data
	Follow up with hospital finance/accounts teams for outstanding payments
	Maintain accurate records of payments received and balances due
	Escalate delays or disputes affecting payment timelines


Stakeholder Management:


	Communicate discrepancies clearly and drive resolution
	Build and maintain strong working relationships with hospital teams
	Provide regular updates on reconciliation and payment status


Reporting and Documentation:


	Prepare weekly reconciliation and variance reports
	Maintain organized, audit-ready documentation
	Provide insights on discrepancies, revenue gaps, and collections


Requirements
Experience


	1 - 3 years of experience in account reconciliation, finance, or audit
	Experience in hospital, pharmacy, or healthcare operations is preferred
	Familiarity with ERP systems is required


Skills and Competencies:


	Strong analytical and reconciliation skills
	High attention to detail and accuracy
	Strong stakeholder management and communication skills
	Proficiency in Excel
	Strong follow-up and problem-solving ability


Salary

Account Reconciliation Officer (Hospital Operations) Job at Castville Solutions




        
    

        
        Castville Solutions is a recruitment and business solutions provider specializing in the healthcare sector. We focus on connecting healthcare facilities with highly skilled professionals, enabling efficient operations, and driving business growth. Our services extend to recruitment, Templates for process and operations management, SOP development, and healthcare-specific training, with a clear mission to transform the healthcare workforce and operations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Account Reconciliation Officer (Hospital Operations)

Job Location: Ibadan, Oyo 
Employment Type: Contract

Role Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Account Reconciliation Officer is responsible for ensuring alignment between company sales data (from the internal ERP system) and hospital client records.
	The role involves generating reports, conducting on-site validation at the hospital, resolving discrepancies, tracking payments, and maintaining accurate reconciliation records.
	The ideal candidate is detail-oriented, experienced in healthcare or hospital reconciliation, and able to manage client stakeholders effectively to ensure timely resolution and payment.


Key Responsibilities


	Data Reconciliation and Validation
	Generate and analyze sales reports from the company’s ERP system
	Perform weekly reconciliation of internal sales data against hospital records
	Visit the hospital to access and verify client-side reports
	Compare both data sources and ensure alignment of revenue, quantities, and billing
	Identify, investigate, and resolve discrepancies
	Document all discrepancies and agreed resolutions


Payment Tracking and Follow-Up


	Track invoices based on reconciled data
	Follow up with hospital finance/accounts teams for outstanding payments
	Maintain accurate records of payments received and balances due
	Escalate delays or disputes affecting payment timelines


Stakeholder Management:


	Communicate discrepancies clearly and drive resolution
	Build and maintain strong working relationships with hospital teams
	Provide regular updates on reconciliation and payment status


Reporting and Documentation:


	Prepare weekly reconciliation and variance reports
	Maintain organized, audit-ready documentation
	Provide insights on discrepancies, revenue gaps, and collections


Requirements
Experience


	1 - 3 years of experience in account reconciliation, finance, or audit
	Experience in hospital, pharmacy, or healthcare operations is preferred
	Familiarity with ERP systems is required


Skills and Competencies:


	Strong analytical and reconciliation skills
	High attention to detail and accuracy
	Strong stakeholder management and communication skills
	Proficiency in Excel
	Strong follow-up and problem-solving ability


Salary
N200,000 - N250,000 / month.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Brand & Marketing Manager",
  "description": "Elysian is a design brand based in Lagos.Our work is defined by engineered embroidery, cutwork technique, and limited production.&nbsp;Our work is defined by engineered embroidery, cutwork technique, and limited production. We dress women who have arrived &mdash; in every sense of the word.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Brand &amp; Marketing Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

The Role


	You will report directly to the founder and be responsible for translating the creative vision of the house into everything the world sees and feels &mdash; across digital, editorial, events, and community. You will work directly with the founder and report to no one else.
	This is not a support role. You will lead.


Core Responsibilities


	Content strategy and social media (Instagram primary)
	Campaign execution for our debut collection and beyond
	Email marketing and CRM (Klaviyo)
	Press outreach and editorial relationships
	Trunk show and event logistics
	Brand partnerships and collaborations
	Community &mdash; our invitation-only seasonal salon


What we are looking for


	You have 4&ndash;6 years of experience in brand or marketing, with at least some of that in fashion, beauty, or premium lifestyle.
	You have a strong visual and editorial sensibility, the ideal candidate should understand the difference between content that sells and content that builds a world.
	You are as comfortable writing a press note as you are briefing a photographer. Y
	ou know Lagos and you understand how luxury speaks here.
	You do not need to have worked at a big house. You need to have cared deeply about the work wherever you have been.


What we offier


	Salary: N400,000 &ndash; N500,000 / month, commensurate with experience
	A house being built from the ground up &mdash; with intention.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send a short note (not a cover letter - just tell us why this, why now), the name of a Nigerian brand whose marketing you admire and why, and two or three examples of work you are proud of to:&nbsp;elysianlagos@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note:&nbsp;Applications without these verifiable details of work will&nbsp;not be reviewed.",

  "validThrough": "2026-04-16",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Elysian"
    
  },

      "addressCountry": "NG"
     
      
    }

Brand & Marketing Manager Job at Elysian




        
    

        
        Elysian is a design brand based in Lagos.Our work is defined by engineered embroidery, cutwork technique, and limited production. Our work is defined by engineered embroidery, cutwork technique, and limited production. We dress women who have arrived — in every sense of the word.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Brand & Marketing Manager

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

The Role
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	You will report directly to the founder and be responsible for translating the creative vision of the house into everything the world sees and feels — across digital, editorial, events, and community. You will work directly with the founder and report to no one else.
	This is not a support role. You will lead.


Core Responsibilities


	Content strategy and social media (Instagram primary)
	Campaign execution for our debut collection and beyond
	Email marketing and CRM (Klaviyo)
	Press outreach and editorial relationships
	Trunk show and event logistics
	Brand partnerships and collaborations
	Community — our invitation-only seasonal salon


What we are looking for


	You have 4–6 years of experience in brand or marketing, with at least some of that in fashion, beauty, or premium lifestyle.
	You have a strong visual and editorial sensibility, the ideal candidate should understand the difference between content that sells and content that builds a world.
	You are as comfortable writing a press note as you are briefing a photographer. Y
	ou know Lagos and you understand how luxury speaks here.
	You do not need to have worked at a big house. You need to have cared deeply about the work wherever you have been.


What we offier


	Salary: N400,000 – N500,000 / month, commensurate with experience
	A house being built from the ground up — with intention.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send a short note (not a cover letter - just tell us why this, why now), the name of a Nigerian brand whose marketing you admire and why, and two or three examples of work you are proud of to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note: Applications without these verifiable details of work will not be reviewed.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

	WEPHCO is seeking a high-performance Graphic Designer / Digital Marketer who can drive visibility, engagement, and lead generation at scale.
